message:
test(ft-kfkyi): 3xx-no-follow companion test with resolvable Location (tick 352)

Added `distributed_http_client_does_not_follow_3xx_even_with_resolvable_location`
— a companion test to `distributed_http_client_returns_3xx_redirect_as_ok_response`
that strengthens the ft-kfkyi no-redirects contract by using a
**resolvable** Location target rather than `does-not-resolve.invalid.`.

Why this matters: the original tick-349 snapshot (now tick-351-flipped
Ok(302) test) uses an unresolvable Location. That proves the client
doesn't burn DNS time on a follow — but it could theoretically pass
even with a latent regression if the "don't follow" check happened
to trigger early on DNS resolution before the bug manifested.

This companion test uses TWO loopback listeners:
- primary: responds 302 → Location pointing at secondary
- secondary: accepts, flips an atomic flag, responds 200 with body
  "followed!"

If the client silently followed the redirect, the secondary would
see an incoming connection AND the resp.body would be b"followed!"
instead of the 302's empty body. Both checks fire.

Also waits 100ms after the request completes to catch a race where a
late follow arrives after the primary response was already read.

Together with the other 3xx test, now pins the contract under both
DNS-fail and DNS-success conditions — the regression surface is
minimal. ft-kfkyi's verification checklist (step 4: "add a companion
test with a resolvable internal redirect target") is now satisfied.

Test result: 1 passed; 0 failed
Compile time: 1m 18s cold.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
